# UC-19.2.31 · Nutanix protection-domain replication lag and RPO compliance

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Reliability, Compliance &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We check that copies of your important data keep arriving at the other site on time, so a fire or power loss would not make you give up more work than the team already agreed in writing. When the line falls behind, we name the data group, the site pair, and who owns the fix so the right people are called, not a random inbox.*

---

## Description

Monitors cross-cluster protection-domain health between a source Nutanix cluster and one or more remote sites (second Nutanix cluster, Metro Availability partner, or Xi Leap) by unifying Prism and Prism Central inventory with per-replication telemetry, remote-site link statistics, and BC/DR policy lookups. It surfaces when observed recovery-point drift exceeds a contracted objective, when Cerebro leaves snapshots pending for too long, and when a Metro pair leaves synchronous replication outside an Enabled state, so a regional failure does not surprise teams with a larger data-loss window than the board-approved DR packet describes.

## Value

Your disaster-recovery program lives or dies on provable RPO, not on a single green cluster health tile. This use case makes each protection domain a billable, ticketed object: operations see which remote link starves bandwidth, which PD breaches policy versus which tier is allowed to slip during a change freeze, and which incidents already have a Prism Central alert attached. The result is defensible compliance evidence, faster handoffs between storage and network teams, and ITSI or service-desk rows that show the last successful Cerebro completion time next to the business owner who must accept residual risk when WAN work runs long.

## Implementation

Deploy Splunk_TA_nutanix for inventory and cluster replication counters, add the 60-second HEC poller for `replications` and a nightly synthetic `rpo_status` stream, publish `nutanix_pd_owner.csv` and `nutanix_remote_site_owner.csv`, and schedule the provided SPL every five minutes with throttle on the triple of cluster, protection domain, and remote site. Map results into ITSI as one entity per protection domain, attach critical breaches to the BC/DR on-call queue with the ticket payload in Step 4, and drive a compliance KPI for percentage of async PDs under contracted RPO while Metro pairs alert only on `metro_state` drift. Tier-1 rows page 24 by 7, tier-3 rows should open a ticket without wake-up when the lookup marks the workload as non-production.

## Evidence

A saved search such as `ss_uc_19_2_31_nutanix_pd_rpo_v1` with CSV or PDF output stored in a restricted `idx_evidence` index proves Splunk received `rpo_seconds_observed`, `rpo_objective_seconds` from the PD-owner lookup, `replication_state` and `last_successful_replication_end_time` from the poller, and `link_state` from the merged remote-site and PC alert stream on the day auditors sampled the control.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab pair of clusters, pause the WAN or block HTTPS between sites long enough for `pending_replication_count` to rise while the HEC poller emits `replication_state=RUNNING` with growing `rpo_seconds_observed` versus a lookup `rpo_objective_seconds` of 900; the saved search should return `rpo_breach_ratio>1` and a `high` or `critical` `severity_bucket` after five minutes, and a synthetic `nutanix:prism_central:alerts` row with Remote Site Unreachable should raise `link_known_down=1` for the same cluster and remote name.

### Negative scenario

For a `METRO_AVAILABILITY` PD with healthy synchronous mirrors, set `rpo_seconds_observed=999999` in a dev feed but `metro_state=Enabled` and expect `severity_bucket=ok` because async RPO math does not apply. For a development tier whose lookup sets `rpo_objective_seconds=86400` and `change_freeze_until` to a future holiday, a modest breach should demote to `medium` or `low` from the freeze clause without paging tier-1 on-call in environments where that policy is active.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites

- Splunk_TA_nutanix 5076 on a collection tier with TCP 443 or 9440 access to every Prism Element URL you poll, and on search heads. Pin TA versions; field renames in minor releases are why the `coalesce` lists in the SPL stay verbose.
- Indexes `hci` and `nutanix` (or a single consolidated `hci` index) with an `nutanix_ops` role that can read them, plus a dedicated HEC token for the custom replications poller. Keep `KV_MODE = json` or `INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json` in a wrapper `props.conf` for the custom sourcetypes.
- Two CSV files in `lookups/`: `nutanix_pd_owner.csv` and `nutanix_remote_site_owner.csv` with the columns defined below, `transforms.conf` stanzas that expose them as `LOOKUP-` or direct `inputlookup` names, and version control in git because BC/DR policy owners approve changes.
- Python 3.9+ on the poller host with `certifi` or OS trust store, ability to run every 60 seconds under systemd or a Windows service account, and secrets stored in a vault; never embed long-lived Prism passwords in a repo. Prefer a dedicated `dr_poller@ad.example.com` with read-only access and rotate keys quarterly.
- BC/DR runbook link stored in the ticketing template so the alert payload can deep-link the human procedure when `severity_bucket` is `high` or `critical`.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection

Install Splunk_TA_nutanix 5076 and enable the modular inputs for `sourcetype=nutanix:protection_domain` and `sourcetype=nutanix:remote_site` for each cluster IP or FQDN. If your TA build supports Prism Central, add the `nutanix:prism_central:alerts` and `nutanix:prism_central:cluster` feeds, or add a small parallel script that `POST /api/nutanix/v3/clusters/list` with a scoped PC API key and HECs the JSON as `sourcetype=nutanix:prism_central:cluster` and alert streams. Authentication at Prism Element is typically HTTP basic over TLS to port 9440, or a session `X-Nutanix-Token` from `POST /PrismGateway/services/rest/v1.0/utils/generate_credential_preview` in automation—pick one method and document it. Prism Central can use a service account password plus two-factor if your org requires step-up, but the poller process must be unattended; many teams issue an API key from My Nutanix instead.

Create `lookups/nutanix_pd_owner.csv` with columns: `pd_name` (as shown in `ncli protection-domain list` or the REST `name` field), `cluster_name` (Prism `clusterName` that matches the TA output), `business_service` (CMDB or application id), `tier` (`tier-1`, `tier-2`, or `tier-3` for alert routing), `rpo_objective_seconds` (contracted BC/DR RPO, integer seconds), `owner_email` (governance contact), and `change_freeze_until` (ISO-8601 or blank). Create `lookups/nutanix_remote_site_owner.csv` with: `cluster_name` (owning source cluster), `remote_site_name` (as in Prism’s remote-site object), `link_min_bandwidth_mbps` (contracted WAN floor), `link_p95_latency_objective_ms` (acceptable RTT p95 in milliseconds for async replication on that path), and `is_metro_partner` (`true` when the site pair is a Metro partner where latency objectives differ from a classic DR site). Wire them with:

```ini
[nutanix_pd_owner]
filename = nutanix_pd_owner.csv
case_sensitive_match = true
min_matches = 0

[nutanix_remote_site_owner]
filename = nutanix_remote_site_owner.csv
case_sensitive_match = true
min_matches = 0
```

The off-the-shelf TA does not include per-snapshot Cerebro progress; add a HEC app with `inputs.conf` that defines a HEC global stanza, then place `props.conf` so `nutanix:protection_domain:replication` is JSON per line, `TRUNCATE` at least 1048576, and `TIME_PREFIX` and `MAX_TIMESTAMP_LOOKAHEAD` tuned to the epoch or ISO fields your poller sets.

Custom poller (Python, schedule every 60 seconds per cluster management endpoint) — the sample uses `REQUESTS` inline comments only where needed; add retries on HTTP 401 and 429. Replace `PRISM_IP`, `PD_LIST`, and `SPLUNK_HEC` with your environment.

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import json, os, ssl, time, urllib.request, urllib.parse
SPLUNK_HEC = "https://indexer:8088/services/collector/event"
HEC_TOKEN  = os.environ.get("SPLUNK_HEC_TOKEN", "")

PRISM      = "https://192.0.2.10:9440/api/nutanix/prism/"
USER, PASS = "admin", os.environ.get("NUTX_PASS")  # use a vault, not a plain env in prod
CYCLE_SEC  = 60
# PD_LIST can come from a cached GET /.../protection_domains/ once per 15 minutes
def pd_names() -> list:
    return ["PD_PROD_01", "PD_PROD_02"]

def get_json(path: str) -> dict:
    # send Accept: application/json, Basic auth, verify TLS
    ...

def hec(sourcetype: str, body: dict, cluster: str) -> None:
    event = {
        "sourcetype": sourcetype,
        "index": "hci",
        "fields": {"cluster_name": cluster},
        "event": body,
    }
    ...

def main() -> None:
    while True:
        for name in pd_names():
            repl = get_json(f"/PrismGateway/services/rest/v2.0/protection_domains/{urllib.parse.quote(name)}/replications/")
            for r in repl.get("entities", []):
                hec(
                    "nutanix:protection_domain:replication",
                    {
                        "pd_name": name,
                        "remote_site_name": r.get("remoteSiteName"),
                        "replication_state": r.get("replicationState"),
                        "start_time": r.get("startTimeUsecs"),
                        "end_time": r.get("endTimeUsecs"),
                        "bytes_transferred": r.get("bytesTransferred"),
                        "bytes_remaining": r.get("bytesRemaining"),
                        "current_transmitted_bytes_per_second": r.get("currentTransmittedBps"),
                    },
                    cluster="HFX-PE-01",
                )
            snap = get_json(f"/PrismGateway/services/rest/v2.0/protection_domains/{urllib.parse.quote(name)}/dr_snapshots/")
            last_ok_usec = 0
            for s in snap.get("entities", []):
                e = s.get("endTimeUsecs") or 0
                st = s.get("replicationState") or "Unknown"
                if st.lower() in ("complete", "done", "kcomplete") and e > last_ok_usec:
                    last_ok_usec = e
            now = time.time()
            observed = None
            if last_ok_usec > 0:
                observed = max(0, int(now - (last_ok_usec / 1_000_000.0)))
            hec(
                "nutanix:protection_domain:rpo_status",
                {
                    "pd_name": name,
                    "cluster_name": "HFX-PE-01",
                    "rpo_seconds_configured": 900,
                    "rpo_seconds_observed": observed,
                    "last_successful_replication_end_time": last_ok_usec,
                    "replication_type": "ASYNC" if not name.startswith("MA-") else "METRO_AVAILABILITY",
                    "health_status": "ok" if (observed is not None and observed < 2 * 900) else "breach",
                },
                cluster="HFX-PE-01",
            )
        time.sleep(CYCLE_SEC)

# entrypoint: call main() under supervisord; ensure logs go to a rotated file and stderr.
```

In `props.conf` add stanzas for `nutanix:protection_domain:replication` and `nutanix:protection_domain:rpo_status` with `KV_MODE = json` or automatic JSON extraction, and a `LINE_BREAKER` that treats each HEC post as a single line event. The TA already maps `sourcetype=nutanix:cluster:metrics` for replication bandwidth; if you only have cluster-level metrics, keep them—Step 2 cross-checks with `eventstats` at cluster scope.

Document that Metro Availability objects must set `replication_type=METRO_AVAILABILITY` in the `rpo_status` feed so the SPL branch that ignores async RPO can fire on `metro_state` instead, using fields from the PD REST or your own `ncli` check.

### Step 2 — Create search and alert

Paste the `spl` field (canonical copy below) into Search, set the time range to 60 minutes, and confirm you see a populated table when data exists. The `multisearch` block unions six feeds: PD metadata, per-remote-site statistics, the HEC replications path, the synthetic rpo status stream, cluster replication-rate metrics, and PC alerts. The first `coalesce` blocks normalize field names. The `eventstats` pass pulls `_cluster` metric rows to every per-site row for bandwidth correlation in dashboards. The `mvexpand` is necessary when `stats` produced multivalued `pd_names` from a rare collision—if you never see more than one value, the command still works. The `inputlookup` joins bind BC/DR policy: `rpo_objective_seconds` and freeze windows from `nutanix_pd_owner.csv`, contracted WAN and latency from `nutanix_remote_site_owner.csv`.

`rpo_breach_ratio` is `rpo_seconds_observed / rpo_objective_seconds` with a guard for zero denominators, but only for non-Metro rows. The `case` macro sets `sev0` with uppercase AND/OR. The `severity_bucket` column applies `change_freeze` demotion: if `change_freeze_until` parses to a future timestamp, a `critical` becomes `medium` and a `high` becomes `low` so a seasonal freeze does not page storage leadership when the business already accepted downtime for that tier, while genuine Metro or replication failures still surface because freeze handling only applies to the async breach ladder the same way the BC/DR council wrote the exception.
The alert’s `where` should fire when `severity_bucket` is in the set (`critical`, `high`) for `tier-1` rows (lookup-driven—extend SPL with a `where tier="tier-1"` in production if you split schedules). The ratio rule encodes: breach beyond twice the contract (`rpo_breach_ratio>2`) is `critical` because the organization has already passed the soft warning band; a single breach (`>1` but not double) is `high`; a backlog with a known-down link is `medium` when latency also exceeds the objective, because network is probably already engaged and duplicate pages add noise. Metro PDs use `metro_state!="Enabled"` in `critical` while ignoring `rpo_breach_ratio` because synchronous replication does not share the same RPO story as async snapshots.

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.31 — Nutanix Protection Domain replication lag and RPO compliance. SOURCES: nutanix:protection_domain, nutanix:remote_site, nutanix:protection_domain:replication, nutanix:protection_domain:rpo_status, nutanix:cluster:metrics, nutanix:prism_central:alerts; LOOKUPS: nutanix_pd_owner, nutanix_remote_site_owner; FREEZE: change_freeze_until.")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) sourcetype="nutanix:protection_domain" earliest=-1h@h
      | eval cluster_name=coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name_cluster, name)
      | eval pd_name=coalesce(pd_name, protectionDomainName, protection_domain, pdName, name)
      | eval remote_site_name=coalesce(remote_site_name, remoteSiteName, remote_site, default_remote_site)
      | eval replication_type=upper(coalesce(replication_type, replicationType, "ASYNC"))
      | eval pending_replication_count=tonumber(coalesce(pending_replication_count, pendingReplicationCount, "0"), 10)
      | eval last_successful_replication_end_time=tonumber(coalesce(last_full_replication_time, lastFullReplicationTime, last_replication_time_usec, "0"), 10)
      | eval link_state=upper(coalesce(link_state, linkState, "UP"))
      | eval metro_state=coalesce(metro_state, metroAvailabilityState, "Enabled")
      | eval src="pdmeta" ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) sourcetype="nutanix:remote_site" earliest=-1h@h
      | eval cluster_name=coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name)
      | eval remote_site_name=coalesce(remote_site_name, remoteSiteName, name, remote_site)
      | eval link_p95_ms=tonumber(coalesce(latency_ms_p95, latencyMsP95, "0"), 10)
      | eval link_state=upper(coalesce(link_state, linkState, "UP"))
      | eval pd_name=coalesce(pd_name, "_rs")
      | eval replication_type="ASYNC"
      | eval src="rsite" ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) sourcetype="nutanix:protection_domain:replication" earliest=-15m@m
      | eval cluster_name=coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name)
      | eval pd_name=coalesce(pd_name, protectionDomainName, protection_domain, name)
      | eval remote_site_name=coalesce(remote_site_name, remoteSiteName, remote_site)
      | eval replication_type=upper(coalesce(replication_type, replicationType, "ASYNC"))
      | eval replication_state=upper(coalesce(replication_state, replicationState, "UNKNOWN"))
      | eval last_successful_replication_end_time=tonumber(coalesce(end_time, endTime, complete_time, "0"), 10)
      | eval src="repl" ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) sourcetype="nutanix:protection_domain:rpo_status" earliest=-15m@m
      | eval cluster_name=coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name)
      | eval pd_name=coalesce(pd_name, protectionDomainName, protection_domain, name)
      | eval remote_site_name=coalesce(remote_site_name, remoteSiteName, remote_site)
      | eval replication_type=upper(coalesce(replication_type, replicationType, "ASYNC"))
      | eval rpo_seconds_observed=tonumber(coalesce(rpo_seconds_observed, rpoSecondsObserved, health_rpo_lag_sec, "0"), 10)
      | eval last_successful_replication_end_time=tonumber(coalesce(last_successful_replication_end_time, lastSuccessEpochUs, "0"), 10)
      | eval metro_state=coalesce(metro_state, metroAvailabilityState, "Enabled")
      | eval src="rpos" ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) sourcetype="nutanix:cluster:metrics" earliest=-15m@m
      | eval cluster_name=coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name)
      | eval repl_out_kbps=tonumber(coalesce(replication_transmitted_bandwidth_kBps, repl_tx_kBps, "0"), 10)
      | eval repl_in_kbps=tonumber(coalesce(replication_received_bandwidth_kBps, repl_rx_kBps, "0"), 10)
      | eval pd_name="_cluster"
      | eval remote_site_name="_cluster"
      | eval src="cmetric" ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) sourcetype="nutanix:prism_central:alerts" earliest=-1h@h
      | eval cluster_name=coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name)
      | eval alert_key=lower(tostring(coalesce(alert_type, title, message, subject, "")))
      | eval pd_name=coalesce(impact_entity, entity_name, related_pd, pdName, "_pcalert")
      | eval remote_site_name=coalesce(remote_site_name, related_remote_site, "_pcalert")
      | eval pca_lag=if(like(alert_key, "%protection%domain%replic%lag%") OR like(alert_key, "protectiondomainreplication%"), 1, 0)
      | eval pca_rsunreach=if(like(alert_key, "%remote%site%unreach%") OR like(alert_key, "%remotesite%"), 1, 0)
      | eval link_state=if(pca_rsunreach=1, "DOWN", null())
      | eval replication_type="ASYNC"
      | eval src="pca" ]
| eval remote_site_name=if(remote_site_name="_cluster" OR remote_site_name="_pcalert", null(), remote_site_name)
| eval cluster_join=coalesce(cluster_name, "unknown")
| eventstats max(repl_out_kbps) as cluster_repl_out_kbps max(repl_in_kbps) as cluster_repl_in_kbps by cluster_join
| stats values(pd_name) as pd_names
    latest(metro_state) as metro_state
    latest(pending_replication_count) as pending_replication_count
    latest(rpo_seconds_observed) as rpo_seconds_observed
    latest(replication_type) as replication_type
    max(replication_state) as replication_state
    max(pca_lag) as pca_lag
    max(pca_rsunreach) as pca_rsunreach
    max(last_successful_replication_end_time) as last_successful_replication_end_time
    latest(link_state) as link_state
    max(link_p95_ms) as link_p95_ms by cluster_name, remote_site_name
| mvexpand pd_names
| rename pd_names as pd_name
| where isnotnull(pd_name) AND pd_name!="_rs" AND pd_name!="_pcalert" AND pd_name!="_cluster" AND isnotnull(cluster_name) AND ( isnotnull(remote_site_name) OR match(pd_name, ".+") )
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name, pd_name
    [| inputlookup nutanix_pd_owner.csv
     | eval cluster_name=coalesce(cluster_name, cluster, ClusterName)
     | eval pd_name=coalesce(pd_name, protection_domain, pd, PD)
     | eval rpo_objective_seconds=tonumber(tostring(rpo_objective_seconds), 10)
     | eval change_freeze_until=tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, ""))
     | fields + cluster_name, pd_name, business_service, tier, rpo_objective_seconds, owner_email, change_freeze_until ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name, remote_site_name
    [| inputlookup nutanix_remote_site_owner.csv
     | eval cluster_name=coalesce(cluster_name, cluster, ClusterName)
     | eval remote_site_name=coalesce(remote_site_name, remoteSiteName, name)
     | eval is_metro_partner=if(match(lower(tostring(is_metro_partner)), "(1|t|y|true)"), 1, 0)
     | eval link_p95_latency_objective_ms=tonumber(tostring(link_p95_latency_objective_ms), 10)
     | eval link_min_bandwidth_mbps=tonumber(tostring(link_min_bandwidth_mbps), 10)
     | fields + cluster_name, remote_site_name, link_min_bandwidth_mbps, link_p95_latency_objective_ms, is_metro_partner ]
| eval is_metro=if(replication_type="METRO_AVAILABILITY" OR replication_type="METRO", 1, 0)
| fillnull value=0 pending_replication_count pca_lag pca_rsunreach is_metro_partner
| eval rpo_breach_ratio=if(is_metro=1, null(), if(isnull(rpo_objective_seconds) OR rpo_objective_seconds<=0 OR isnull(rpo_seconds_observed), null(), rpo_seconds_observed / rpo_objective_seconds))
| eval link_known_down=if(pca_rsunreach=1 OR match(tostring(link_state), "^(DOWN|UNREACHABLE)"), 1, 0)
| eval latobj=if(isnotnull(link_p95_latency_objective_ms), link_p95_latency_objective_ms, 99999)
| eval cft1=strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z")
| eval cft2=strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval cft3=strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
| eval cft4=strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval freeze_active=if(isnotnull(cft1) OR isnotnull(cft2) OR isnotnull(cft3) OR isnotnull(cft4), if(now()<coalesce(cft1, cft2, cft3, cft4, 0), 1, 0), 0)
| eval sev0=case(
    is_metro=1 AND ( metro_state!="Enabled" AND isnotnull(metro_state) ), "critical",
    is_metro=1, "ok",
    isnotnull(replication_state) AND ( replication_state="FAILED" OR replication_state="ABORTED" OR replication_state="ERROR" ), "critical",
    link_state="DOWN" OR pca_rsunreach=1, "critical",
    pca_lag=1, "high",
    isnotnull(rpo_breach_ratio) AND rpo_breach_ratio>2, "critical",
    isnotnull(rpo_breach_ratio) AND rpo_breach_ratio>1, "high",
    ( pending_replication_count>0 ) AND ( link_p95_ms>latobj ) AND ( link_known_down=1 ), "medium",
    isnotnull(rpo_breach_ratio) AND rpo_breach_ratio<=1 AND ( pending_replication_count=0 ) AND ( link_known_down=0 ), "ok",
    isnotnull(rpo_breach_ratio) AND rpo_breach_ratio<=1, "low",
    1=1, "low" )
| eval severity_bucket=if( freeze_active=1 AND ( sev0="critical" OR sev0="high" ), if(sev0="critical", "medium", "low"), sev0 )
| table cluster_name, pd_name, remote_site_name, replication_type, rpo_seconds_observed, rpo_objective_seconds, rpo_breach_ratio, last_successful_replication_end_time, replication_state, link_state, severity_bucket, freeze_active, business_service, tier, owner_email
```

`savedsearches.conf` (merge into the customer wrapper app) example:

```ini
[ss_uc_19_2_31_nutanix_pd_rpo_v1]
action.email = 1
action.email.to = bc_dr_oncall@example.com
alert.track = 1
counttype = number of events
dispatch.earliest_time = -5m
dispatch.latest_time = now
disable = 0
is_scheduled = 1
alert.suppress = 1
alert.suppress.fields = cluster_name, pd_name, remote_site_name
alert.suppress.period = 20m
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
enableSched = 1
quantity = 0
relation = greater than
search = <paste entire SPL from the spl field here>
```

The twenty-minute `alert.suppress.period` with keys `(cluster_name,pd_name,remote_site_name)` blocks duplicate tickets while a WAN team works, but the keys reset immediately if the `severity_bucket` escalates to `critical` in a new run, because Splunk re-evaluates the whole search. Use `dispatch.earliest_time = -5m` on the saved search to match the poller, while the multisearch still carries its own `earliest` inside each subsearch. Add a workload JSON action to push `pd_name`, `cluster_name`, `remote_site_name`, `rpo_seconds_observed`, and `change_freeze_until` into the ticket field pack.

### Step 3 — Validate

On day one, run `| tstats count where (index=hci OR index=nutanix) sourcetype=nutanix:protection_domain by sourcetype` to prove the TA is ingesting, then the same for each custom sourcetype. Run `| inputlookup nutanix_pd_owner.csv` and `| inputlookup nutanix_remote_site_owner.csv` to confirm CSV headers match the join keys, because a stray space in ` cluster_name` breaks the join silently. For RPO plausibility, add `| eval chk=if(rpo_seconds_observed<0 OR rpo_seconds_observed>7*24*3600, "bad", "ok")` in a one-off; negative values usually mean a microsecond timestamp treated as seconds. Run `| stats max(last_successful_replication_end_time) as hi min(last_successful_replication_end_time) as lo` and confirm the hi/lo range matches epoch microseconds if your poller used Usecs—if the max is 1E12, convert with `/1000/1000` in the HEC, not in the search. Pick one Metro PD, compare `ncli pd ls` on a CVM to the same `replication_type` in Splunk, and confirm the search sets `is_metro=1` so the async ladder does not page. This Step 3 checklist is unique to this PD cross-site pattern: the proof is about dual-site Cerebro telemetry and lookup governance, not generic CPU or PC-only feeds.

### Step 4 — Operationalize

Publish a dashboard: heatmap as described in `visualization`, a backlog timechart, success/fail bars from `replication_state`, a bandwidth line chart, and a sync versus async count panel. Enable drilldown tokens so a click on a cell opens a detail tab with the raw `nutanix:protection_domain:replication` event and the `remote_site` row for the same minute. In ITSI, define one service entity per `pd_name` and bind KPIs for `100 - (breach count)` style compliance and a secondary KPI for `sum(bytes_remaining)` from the HEC. Route `critical` to the BC/DR bridge and `high` to the Nutanix platform queue; attach the ticket template fields: `pd_name`, `cluster_name`, `remote_site_name`, `rpo_breach_ratio`, and `last_successful_replication_end_time` in epoch for Splunk, plus a human-readable ISO field generated with `fieldformat`. Link the escalation play to the org’s RTO runbook, not to the general hypervisor SOP. For tier-1 `business_service` values, set PagerDuty high urgency; for `tier-3` use email unless `change_freeze_until` has expired. Document that the heatmap is source-cluster by remote-site, not by VM, to avoid conflating this metric with per-guest recovery objectives.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting

(1) Missing `nutanix:protection_domain:replication` events: the HEC poller or its systemd unit stopped, Prism credentials rolled, or TLS interception broke—check `/var/log/dr_pd_poller.log` and `splunkd.log` for HEC 401/403, then test `curl` to `/PrismGateway/services/rest/v2.0/protection_domains/` on the same host.
(2) `rpo_seconds_observed` stuck at `0` while Prism shows drift: the poller is subtracting a microsecond `last_full_replication_time` as if it were seconds; divide by one million in Python before you compute the delta to `time.time`.
(3) Metro PDs with enormous apparent lag: exclude async math—confirm `replication_type` matches `METRO_AVAILABILITY` in the rpo status feed, then verify `is_metro=1` rows in the `eval` output; if not, a mis-tagged HEC line is the culprit. Sync partners should alert only when `metro_state` leaves Enabled, not on snapshot backlog, because Cerebro snapshot queues for Metro are not the same as async RPO minutes.
(4) The remote site still shows up even though the WAN is hard-down: Prism’s remote-site check can be three to five minutes behind line-rate ICMP; pull your SD-WAN or switch telemetry in a parallel panel and, if the carrier owns the last mile, add their incident ticket id to the payload when `link_known_down=1` but `link_state` in Splunk is still up.
(5) One PD is always in breach because the lookup marks `tier-3` with a permanent `change_freeze_until` during a decommission: update the row or set `rpo_objective_seconds` to the relaxed number the steering committee signed; this UC is reporting faithfully even when the business chose not to pay for a tighter SLA.
(6) HEC throttling: if `splunkd.log` on the poller host shows 429, adjust `max_global_raw_size` in `limits.conf` on the indexers, split tokens per cluster, or back off the cycle to ninety seconds, because a full-site snapshot storm can outrun default per-token throughput during Curator work.
(7) Ingest queue on the HEC: when `_indextime - _time` for these sourcetypes exceeds a few minutes, the alert will show stale Cerebro time—scale the Heavy Forwarder worker threads or add another indexer peer before tuning thresholds.
(8) `mvexpand` error: if `values(pd_name)` never lists more than one `pd_name`, the command is harmless, but an upgrade that returns multivalues might require you to add `mvexpand` limits—set `mvexpand_max` in the search string if a cluster with hundreds of PDs hits a cap.
(9) `inputlookup` miss: verify both CSV files use the same Unicode normalization for `remote_site_name` (ASCII hyphen versus en-dash) as Prism, because a mismatch drives `link_p95_ms` to null and breaks the `medium` case.
(10) PC alerts missing: confirm the PC user account you used for the TA can read the alert API; some tenants disable PC alerts in favor of only PE events—re-enable or duplicate the `ProtectionDomainReplicationLag` alert in PC so the `pca_lag` flag can trip.


## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.31 — Nutanix Protection Domain replication lag and RPO compliance. SOURCES: nutanix:protection_domain, nutanix:remote_site, nutanix:protection_domain:replication, nutanix:protection_domain:rpo_status, nutanix:cluster:metrics, nutanix:prism_central:alerts; LOOKUPS: nutanix_pd_owner, nutanix_remote_site_owner; FREEZE: change_freeze_until.")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) sourcetype="nutanix:protection_domain" earliest=-1h@h
      | eval cluster_name=coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name_cluster, name)
      | eval pd_name=coalesce(pd_name, protectionDomainName, protection_domain, pdName, name)
      | eval remote_site_name=coalesce(remote_site_name, remoteSiteName, remote_site, default_remote_site)
      | eval replication_type=upper(coalesce(replication_type, replicationType, "ASYNC"))
      | eval pending_replication_count=tonumber(coalesce(pending_replication_count, pendingReplicationCount, "0"), 10)
      | eval last_successful_replication_end_time=tonumber(coalesce(last_full_replication_time, lastFullReplicationTime, last_replication_time_usec, "0"), 10)
      | eval link_state=upper(coalesce(link_state, linkState, "UP"))
      | eval metro_state=coalesce(metro_state, metroAvailabilityState, "Enabled")
      | eval src="pdmeta" ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) sourcetype="nutanix:remote_site" earliest=-1h@h
      | eval cluster_name=coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name)
      | eval remote_site_name=coalesce(remote_site_name, remoteSiteName, name, remote_site)
      | eval link_p95_ms=tonumber(coalesce(latency_ms_p95, latencyMsP95, "0"), 10)
      | eval link_state=upper(coalesce(link_state, linkState, "UP"))
      | eval pd_name=coalesce(pd_name, "_rs")
      | eval replication_type="ASYNC"
      | eval src="rsite" ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) sourcetype="nutanix:protection_domain:replication" earliest=-15m@m
      | eval cluster_name=coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name)
      | eval pd_name=coalesce(pd_name, protectionDomainName, protection_domain, name)
      | eval remote_site_name=coalesce(remote_site_name, remoteSiteName, remote_site)
      | eval replication_type=upper(coalesce(replication_type, replicationType, "ASYNC"))
      | eval replication_state=upper(coalesce(replication_state, replicationState, "UNKNOWN"))
      | eval last_successful_replication_end_time=tonumber(coalesce(end_time, endTime, complete_time, "0"), 10)
      | eval src="repl" ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) sourcetype="nutanix:protection_domain:rpo_status" earliest=-15m@m
      | eval cluster_name=coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name)
      | eval pd_name=coalesce(pd_name, protectionDomainName, protection_domain, name)
      | eval remote_site_name=coalesce(remote_site_name, remoteSiteName, remote_site)
      | eval replication_type=upper(coalesce(replication_type, replicationType, "ASYNC"))
      | eval rpo_seconds_observed=tonumber(coalesce(rpo_seconds_observed, rpoSecondsObserved, health_rpo_lag_sec, "0"), 10)
      | eval last_successful_replication_end_time=tonumber(coalesce(last_successful_replication_end_time, lastSuccessEpochUs, "0"), 10)
      | eval metro_state=coalesce(metro_state, metroAvailabilityState, "Enabled")
      | eval src="rpos" ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) sourcetype="nutanix:cluster:metrics" earliest=-15m@m
      | eval cluster_name=coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name)
      | eval repl_out_kbps=tonumber(coalesce(replication_transmitted_bandwidth_kBps, repl_tx_kBps, "0"), 10)
      | eval repl_in_kbps=tonumber(coalesce(replication_received_bandwidth_kBps, repl_rx_kBps, "0"), 10)
      | eval pd_name="_cluster"
      | eval remote_site_name="_cluster"
      | eval src="cmetric" ]
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) sourcetype="nutanix:prism_central:alerts" earliest=-1h@h
      | eval cluster_name=coalesce(cluster_name, clusterName, name)
      | eval alert_key=lower(tostring(coalesce(alert_type, title, message, subject, "")))
      | eval pd_name=coalesce(impact_entity, entity_name, related_pd, pdName, "_pcalert")
      | eval remote_site_name=coalesce(remote_site_name, related_remote_site, "_pcalert")
      | eval pca_lag=if(like(alert_key, "%protection%domain%replic%lag%") OR like(alert_key, "protectiondomainreplication%"), 1, 0)
      | eval pca_rsunreach=if(like(alert_key, "%remote%site%unreach%") OR like(alert_key, "%remotesite%"), 1, 0)
      | eval link_state=if(pca_rsunreach=1, "DOWN", null())
      | eval replication_type="ASYNC"
      | eval src="pca" ]
| eval remote_site_name=if(remote_site_name="_cluster" OR remote_site_name="_pcalert", null(), remote_site_name)
| eval cluster_join=coalesce(cluster_name, "unknown")
| eventstats max(repl_out_kbps) as cluster_repl_out_kbps max(repl_in_kbps) as cluster_repl_in_kbps by cluster_join
| stats values(pd_name) as pd_names
    latest(metro_state) as metro_state
    latest(pending_replication_count) as pending_replication_count
    latest(rpo_seconds_observed) as rpo_seconds_observed
    latest(replication_type) as replication_type
    max(replication_state) as replication_state
    max(pca_lag) as pca_lag
    max(pca_rsunreach) as pca_rsunreach
    max(last_successful_replication_end_time) as last_successful_replication_end_time
    latest(link_state) as link_state
    max(link_p95_ms) as link_p95_ms by cluster_name, remote_site_name
| mvexpand pd_names
| rename pd_names as pd_name
| where isnotnull(pd_name) AND pd_name!="_rs" AND pd_name!="_pcalert" AND pd_name!="_cluster" AND isnotnull(cluster_name) AND ( isnotnull(remote_site_name) OR match(pd_name, ".+") )
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name, pd_name
    [| inputlookup nutanix_pd_owner.csv
     | eval cluster_name=coalesce(cluster_name, cluster, ClusterName)
     | eval pd_name=coalesce(pd_name, protection_domain, pd, PD)
     | eval rpo_objective_seconds=tonumber(tostring(rpo_objective_seconds), 10)
     | eval change_freeze_until=tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, ""))
     | fields + cluster_name, pd_name, business_service, tier, rpo_objective_seconds, owner_email, change_freeze_until ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name, remote_site_name
    [| inputlookup nutanix_remote_site_owner.csv
     | eval cluster_name=coalesce(cluster_name, cluster, ClusterName)
     | eval remote_site_name=coalesce(remote_site_name, remoteSiteName, name)
     | eval is_metro_partner=if(match(lower(tostring(is_metro_partner)), "(1|t|y|true)"), 1, 0)
     | eval link_p95_latency_objective_ms=tonumber(tostring(link_p95_latency_objective_ms), 10)
     | eval link_min_bandwidth_mbps=tonumber(tostring(link_min_bandwidth_mbps), 10)
     | fields + cluster_name, remote_site_name, link_min_bandwidth_mbps, link_p95_latency_objective_ms, is_metro_partner ]
| eval is_metro=if(replication_type="METRO_AVAILABILITY" OR replication_type="METRO", 1, 0)
| fillnull value=0 pending_replication_count pca_lag pca_rsunreach is_metro_partner
| eval rpo_breach_ratio=if(is_metro=1, null(), if(isnull(rpo_objective_seconds) OR rpo_objective_seconds<=0 OR isnull(rpo_seconds_observed), null(), rpo_seconds_observed / rpo_objective_seconds))
| eval link_known_down=if(pca_rsunreach=1 OR match(tostring(link_state), "^(DOWN|UNREACHABLE)"), 1, 0)
| eval latobj=if(isnotnull(link_p95_latency_objective_ms), link_p95_latency_objective_ms, 99999)
| eval cft1=strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z")
| eval cft2=strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval cft3=strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
| eval cft4=strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval freeze_active=if(isnotnull(cft1) OR isnotnull(cft2) OR isnotnull(cft3) OR isnotnull(cft4), if(now()<coalesce(cft1, cft2, cft3, cft4, 0), 1, 0), 0)
| eval sev0=case(
    is_metro=1 AND ( metro_state!="Enabled" AND isnotnull(metro_state) ), "critical",
    is_metro=1, "ok",
    isnotnull(replication_state) AND ( replication_state="FAILED" OR replication_state="ABORTED" OR replication_state="ERROR" ), "critical",
    link_state="DOWN" OR pca_rsunreach=1, "critical",
    pca_lag=1, "high",
    isnotnull(rpo_breach_ratio) AND rpo_breach_ratio>2, "critical",
    isnotnull(rpo_breach_ratio) AND rpo_breach_ratio>1, "high",
    ( pending_replication_count>0 ) AND ( link_p95_ms>latobj ) AND ( link_known_down=1 ), "medium",
    isnotnull(rpo_breach_ratio) AND rpo_breach_ratio<=1 AND ( pending_replication_count=0 ) AND ( link_known_down=0 ), "ok",
    isnotnull(rpo_breach_ratio) AND rpo_breach_ratio<=1, "low",
    1=1, "low" )
| eval severity_bucket=if( freeze_active=1 AND ( sev0="critical" OR sev0="high" ), if(sev0="critical", "medium", "low"), sev0 )
| table cluster_name, pd_name, remote_site_name, replication_type, rpo_seconds_observed, rpo_objective_seconds, rpo_breach_ratio, last_successful_replication_end_time, replication_state, link_state, severity_bucket, freeze_active, business_service, tier, owner_email
```

## Visualization

Build a first-row heatmap of source `cluster_name` on the Y axis and `remote_site_name` on the X axis colored by `severity_bucket` for each `pd_name`, a second row with a timechart of `rpo_seconds_observed` versus `rpo_objective_seconds` and stacked overlay of `bytes_remaining` from the HEC poller, a bar chart of snapshot or replication success versus failure by hour, a third row for pair charts of `cluster_repl_out_kbps` against `link_min_bandwidth_mbps` from the remote-site owner lookup, and a summary panel counting async versus Metro protection domains. Wire drilldowns to the per-PD runbook, to remote-site network dashboards, and to a raw `nutanix:prism_central:alerts` event view filtered on the same `pd_name` token.

## Known False Positives

A planned re-protect or re-seed after storage expansion can force Cerebro to ship a full fresh snapshot, so `rpo_seconds_observed` balloons for hours with an approved change record even though the environment is under control. Spring or autumn daylight-saving jumps can shift the comparison between epoch milliseconds emitted by the poller and `now()` by one wall-clock hour, producing a one-off false breach until NTP and locale settings align on the collector. During a scheduled Metro site survivability test, administrators toggle Metro Availability; Splunk can briefly list `metro_state` outside Enabled even though the async leg is irrelevant to that test window. A Curator deep scrub that pauses background replication to protect foreground I/O can spike `pending_replication_count` for a few intervals without a WAN fault—cross-check CVM Curator throttling in Prism before you blame the link. If snapshot retention expires a remote snapshot in the same minute a poller sample runs, you may see a momentary `pending_replication_count` tick that clears on the next Cerebro pass. HYCU- or Veeam-driven temporary PD pauses for application-consistent quiesce are outside this index and can look like silence until you read the application backup runbook. Finally, a mis-keyed `rpo_objective_seconds` entry of zero in the lookup makes `rpo_breach_ratio` null and can hide a row during QA until CSV governance catches the typo—treat that as a data-quality alert, not a storage outage.

## References

- [Splunk Add-on for Nutanix Prism (Splunkbase 5076)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5076)
- [Nutanix — Protection Domains, Metro Availability, and DR (Prism Web Console guide)](https://portal.nutanix.com/)
- [Nutanix Developer — Prism API v2 and v3 reference](https://www.nutanix.dev/)
